title: taxus cuspidata subsp. biternata
intro: Taxus cuspidata subsp. biternata, also known as the Japanese yew, is a subspecies of the Japanese yew tree, native to East Asia. This evergreen conifer has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially inhibiting the growth of cancer cells. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested for its leaves and bark, which are then dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's essential to exercise caution and consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
